tweets: Your Korean Saju chart is just 8 characters arranged in 4 pillars. Here's how to read yours in plain English. 🧵

1/ The 4 pillars:

• YEAR — ancestry, early childhood, social tribe
• MONTH — career, parents, public self
• DAY — your core self + spouse
• HOUR — children, late life, hidden talents

Each pillar = 1 stem on top + 1 branch below.
2/ Your Day Master = top character of your day pillar. It's THE most-read character in any Saju reading. There are only 10 possibilities — find yours and 80% of your chart is already decoded.

3/ Yang Wood (甲) = tall pine, idealist, founder.
Yin Water (癸) = mist, intuitive, sensitive.
Yang Fire (丙) = sun, charismatic, public.
Each Day Master has a distinct character.
4/ Then the Five Elements — Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, Water — count how many of each appear across your 8 characters. That balance (or imbalance) tells you what your chart needs.

5/ Finally, the Sip-sin (Ten Gods) labels every element relative to your Day Master:

• Resource = your support, mother, learning
• Wealth = your earnings, romance (if male)
• Officer = your structure, career, husband (if female)

This is where Saju gets predictive.

Calculate your eight characters free (English):
https://cheonmyeongdang.vercel.app/en/four-pillars-of-destiny.html

Reply with your Day Master if you find it.
bluesky_short:
Your Korean Saju chart = 8 characters in 4 pillars (year/month/day/hour). The single most-read character = your Day Master.

Free English calculator: https://cheonmyeongdang.vercel.app/en/four-pillars-of-destiny.html
